Speaking to Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec on Bloomberg Businessweek, Holcomb explains that gaining support from big tech and leading AI companies like Amazon, Anthropic and Microsoft shows that shared commitment to preparing workers for AI-driven jobs.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Stocks Set for Best Quarter Since Pandemic; SCOTUS Expands Trump's Power | Bloomberg Brief 6/30/2026

A divided US Supreme Court expands President Trump's power to fire top government officials at federal agencies, while reinforcing the Federal Reserve's independence and protecting the position of Fed Governor Lisa Cook. Jack Caffrey of JPMorgan Asset Management discusses the second quarter.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Iran Ratchets Up Talk of Controlling Hormuz Before New Talks | The Opening Trade 6/30/2026
title_cn: 伊朗在新会谈之前加强控制霍尔木兹海峡的讨论开盘交易 2026 年 6 月 30 日
summary:
Iran reiterated its determination to control maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, raising the stakes ahead of fresh talks in Qatar on formally ending its war with the US.

Speaking to state television, Deputy Foreign Minister Kazem Gharibabadi said Iran wants to work out an agreement with Oman, which borders the southern side of the vital waterway, to oversee ships passing through Hormuz. But Iran will move forward with its own plans “if for any reason Oman is not interested in doing so,” he said.

The Opening Trade has everything you need to know as markets open across Europe. With analysis you won't find anywhere else, we break down the biggest stories of the day and speak to top guests who have skin in the game. Hosted by Anna Edwards, Guy Johnson and Tom Mackenzie. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Meiji Yasuda Doubles Super-Long Japanese Bond Buying Plan
title_cn: 明治安田将超长期日本债券购买计划加倍
summary: Meiji Yasuda Life Insurance Co. aims to buy more than ¥2 trillion ($12 billion) of super-long Japanese government bonds in the fiscal year ending March 2027, doubling its earlier purchase plan on the view that yields have climbed to desirable levels.

title: Iran Demands Greater Control of Hormuz as Talks Resume
title_cn: 随着谈判恢复，伊朗要求加强对霍尔木兹的控制
summary: Iran reiterated its determination to oversee and control maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz ahead of a fresh round of talks in Qatar on formally ending its war with the US. Tyler Kendall reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Europe Regulator Recognizes India Sovereign Bond Clearing House
title_cn: 欧洲监管机构认可印度主权债券清算所
summary: European authorities formally recognized India’s sovereign debt clearinghouse as a permitted trading counterparty, making it easier for lenders like Deutsche Bank AG and BNP Paribas SA to trade bonds in the Asian nation.

Alaric Nightengale reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: BOE’s Breeden Says AI Agents Could Amplify Market Stress

She speaks at the European Central Bank's annual symposium in Sintra, Portugal. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: AI Notetakers in Meetings Raise Mounting Privacy Concerns

title: IMF: Cyberattacks Getting Faster
title_cn: 国际货币基金组织：网络攻击速度越来越快
summary:
The time between a software vulnerability being found and then exploited by a bad actor is shrinking says Tobias Adrian, Financial Counsellor and Director of the Monetary and Capital Markets Department at the International Monetary Fund. This poses a real threat to financial stability, says Adrian. He spoke to Bloomberg's Francine Lacqua on the sidelines of the ECB Forum on Central Banking in Sintra on 'The Pulse'. 
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Lee’s $880 Billion AI Bet Ties Legacy to South Korea Chip Boom
title_cn: Lee 的 8800 亿美元 AI 赌注将遗产与韩国芯片热潮联系在一起
summary: With South Korea already one of the biggest winners of the global AI boom, President Lee Jae Myung is now staking his legacy on a plan to transform the nation’s less developed southwest into a global chip hub.

title: Weak Hiring Is Hurting Young Workers More than AI, Study Says
title_cn: 研究称，招聘疲软对年轻工人的伤害比人工智能更大
summary: A shortage of job openings in the US is playing a much bigger role in young people’s unemployment than their lack of artificial intelligence skills, according to a new study from the Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis.

title: SAP Gives AI Product Oversight to Top Executives in Reshuffle

summary: SAP SE is divvying up responsibilities for its product and engineering functions in its second top-level reorganization this year, people familiar with the matter said, as Europe’s largest software company grapples with staying ahead of rising artificial intelligence competition.

title: The Original Prediction Market Boosters Have Qualms About the Boom
title_cn: 最初的预测市场推动者对繁荣抱有疑虑
summary: At Manifest, some tech intellectuals wondered whether Super Bowl bets were really helping humanity.

title: What the Saga Over Anthropic’s Mythos Tells Us About the Cyber Risks From AI
title_cn: 关于人类神话的传奇故事告诉我们关于人工智能的网络风险
summary: Anthropic PBC has said its Mythos artificial intelligence model is so good at finding vulnerabilities in software and computer systems that it can’t be released to the general public. As of early June, the AI giant had released Mythos to only 200 partner organizations because if a tool this powerful fell into the wrong hands, Anthropic said, it could help attackers more easily steal data or disrupt critical infrastructure.

title: Citi’s Chew Sees Selloff Risk in Tech Stocks From Positioning

title: M&G's Fedeli Says Its Worth Investing in 'AI Losers'

summary: Fabiana Fedeli, CIO of multi-asset at M&G Investments, says that while there is still value in equities, markets such as South Korea have displayed "exuberant" behavior. Speaking on Bloomberg Television, she says: "there is a whole host of stocks which are the AI losers — the stocks that have been forgotten by the AI trade — that I still think I still think are very worthwhile investing in." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: NeoPulse-Led Group to Acquire Stake in Korean Game Maker Wemade

title: Amazon to Pay $2.25 Million to End US Case Over ID Theft Victims

title: StanChart Former AI Chief Joins Accenture as Southeast Asia Head

title: South Korea to Speed Nuclear Push to Meet Surging Power Demand
title_cn: 韩国将加快核计划以满足不断增长的电力需求
summary: The South Korean government will consider ways to cut atomic power construction times as it looks to ramp up energy supply to meet the demands of artificial intelligence.

title: Tencent Ramps Up Buybacks as Share Rout Wipes Out $309 Billion
title_cn: 腾讯加大回购力度，股价暴跌损失 3090 亿美元
summary: China’s Tencent Holdings Ltd. is ramping up buybacks as its Hong Kong-listed shares struggle to recover from a selloff that’s wiped out about $309 billion in market value since early October.

title: House Passes Online Safety Bill as Senate Eyes Stricter Push
title_cn: 众议院通过在线安全法案，参议院加大力度推动
summary: The House on Monday passed legislation to require new online safety protections for children, a sign of growing momentum in Washington to address a widespread concern among US parents even as the measure sets up a clash with senators seeking stronger safeguards.

title: Amazon Sued by Australian Consumer Watchdog Over Prime Video Ads
title_cn: 亚马逊因 Prime 视频广告被澳大利亚消费者监管机构起诉
summary: Amazon.com Inc. has been sued by Australia’s consumer watchdog for introducing advertising to its Prime Video streaming service and then forcing existing subscribers to pay more to avoid the ads.

title: Bessent Says Only China Buying Iranian Oil, Others Are Wary
title_cn: 贝森特称只有中国购买伊朗石油，其他国家持谨慎态度
summary: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said that most nations aren’t yet ready to purchase Iranian oil, wary over the potential of the US reimposing sanctions, and that this is an incentive for Tehran to negotiate with Washington.

title: Witkoff, Kushner Set to Hold Indirect Talks With Iran in Qatar
title_cn: 威特科夫和库什纳将在卡塔尔与伊朗举行间接会谈
summary: Qatar said Jared Kushner and Steve Witkoff had arrived in Doha as part of ongoing peace negotiations between the US and Iran and as the countries try to calm tensions over the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Trump Pauses Moroccan Fertilizer Duties to Aid Farm Economy
title_cn: 特朗普暂停摩洛哥化肥税以援助农业经济
summary: President Donald Trump will temporarily lift duties on phosphate fertilizer from Morocco as the administration seeks to aid a struggling farm economy and ease consumer fears over food inflation.

title: France’s Far Right Joins the Fray of Air Conditioning Politics
title_cn: 法国极右翼加入空调政治的纷争
summary: France’s far right is promising air conditioners now, rather than climate solutions for later, to try and sway voters as Europe bakes under record temperatures.

title: Starmer Says Defense Plan Allows Burnham to Counter UK Threats
title_cn: 斯塔默表示，国防计划使伯纳姆能够应对英国的威胁
summary: Prime Minister Keir Starmer said he was confident his likely successor, Andy Burnham, would maintain the UK’s commitment to defense, as he laid out plans to invest an extra £15 billion ($20 billion) to modernize the British military.

title: Bundesbank Joins Race to Run Germany’s Sprawling Pension Fund
title_cn: 德国央行加入管理德国庞大养老基金的竞赛
summary: Germany’s central bank is vying to oversee the country’s planned state-backed pension fund, set to be one of Europe’s largest pools of long-term capital worth hundreds of billions of euros.

title: Anti-Migrant Protests Draw Thousands Across South Africa
title_cn: 南非各地数千人举行反移民抗议活动
summary: Thousands of people joined anti-migrant protests in South Africa on Tuesday, watched over by a massive police contingent that was deployed to prevent violence and intimidation.

title: How Trump Has Changed the American Dream for Many Immigrants
title_cn: 特朗普如何改变了许多移民的美国梦
summary: The president is pushing to redefine who is welcome — and who isn’t.

title: Superyacht Linked to Usmanov Ruled Not Under EU Sanctions
title_cn: 与乌斯马诺夫有关的超级游艇不受欧盟制裁
summary: A superyacht linked to Russian billionaire Alisher Usmanov’s family can no longer be considered frozen under the European Union’s sanctions regime, a Frankfurt court ruled, allowing the shipyard maintaining the vessel to seek payment for years of maintenance.

title: Monaco Bomb Blast Wounds Businessman Sanctioned by Ukraine
title_cn: 摩纳哥炸弹爆炸致商人被乌克兰制裁
summary: A bomb explosion on Monday night in Monaco wounded Vadym Iermolaiev, a local resident who was sanctioned by Ukraine for doing business in occupied Crimea, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Gojek Founder Makarim Found Guilty in Indonesia Chromebooks Case
title_cn: Gojek 创始人 Makarim 在印度尼西亚 Chromebook 案中被判有罪
summary: Former Indonesian Education Minister and Gojek co-founder Nadiem Makarim was found guilty of corruption over a pandemic-era procurement of Chromebooks for schools, a ruling likely to add to investor concerns about how Southeast Asia’s largest economy distinguishes graft from disputed policy and business decisions.

title: Tokyo and Seoul Bet the House on AI Buildouts
title_cn: 东京和首尔押注于人工智能建设
summary: South Korea, Japan unveil massive investment blueprints.

title: Putin’s Closest Ally Tested as Minsk Resists Deeper War Role
title_cn: 明斯克抵制更深层次的战争角色，普京最亲密的盟友面临考验
summary: Russia’s close alliance with Belarus is facing a new test as President Alexander Lukashenko has shown reluctance to deepen his role in Vladimir Putin’s war against Ukraine.

title: Russian Threat Likely to Endure After Putin, Sweden Says
title_cn: 瑞典称，普京之后俄罗斯的威胁可能会持续下去
summary: The Russian regime is likely to remain a threat to its neighbors well after President Vladimir Putin’s time in office ends, the head of Sweden’s military intelligence agency said.

title: Orban’s Ex-Bodyguard Questioned on Ukraine Money Convoy Grab
title_cn: 欧尔班前保镖因乌克兰抢钱案接受质询
summary: Hungarian prosecutors questioned a former top police officer and long-time associate of ex-Prime Minister Viktor Orban on suspicion of having been involved in seizing a Ukraine-bound money transport and unlawfully detaining the officials accompanying it.

title: Congo Orders Cobalt Miners to Give Up Unused Export Quotas
title_cn: 刚果命令钴矿商放弃未使用的出口配额
summary: Cobalt miners in the Democratic Republic of Congo will be required to surrender unused export quotas for the first half of the year to a government regulator, potentially adding to tightness in the market for the battery metal.

title: Lebanon-Israel Pact Fragile After Hezbollah’s Vow of Disruption
title_cn: 真主党誓言破坏后，黎巴嫩-以色列条约变得脆弱
summary: Israel and Lebanon’s latest peace deal faces an uncertain future after Hezbollah, Iran’s most powerful proxy militant group, this week vowed to derail its implementation.

title: Burnham’s Devolution Plan Lacks Detail on Financial Matters
title_cn: 伯纳姆的权力下放计划缺乏财务问题的细节
summary: Andy Burnham’s economic plan for Britain starts from the bottom up.

title: German, Dutch Militaries Take Command on NATO’s Eastern Flank
title_cn: 德国、荷兰军队在北约东翼指挥
summary: Germany and the Netherlands established a military command center in the Baltic region to deter Russia as part of a broader effort to take on responsibility within NATO ahead of a summit of the alliance next week.

title: UK Bets on Drones and AI in Contentious Defense Funding Plan
title_cn: 英国在有争议的国防资助计划中押注无人机和人工智能
summary: The UK will seek to transform its aging military into a force fit for modern warfare by investing in drones and artificial intelligence alongside more traditional deterrents such as fighter jets and nuclear weapons.

title: UK to Update Military Capabilities Using Drones, AI
title_cn: 英国将利用无人机和人工智能更新军事能力
summary: The UK is updating its military by investing in new technology like drones and artificial intelligence, while still keeping traditional deterrents like fighter jets and nuclear weapons. Prime Minister Keir Starmer will lay out the plans on Tuesday after months of delay and a funding row. Bloomberg's Lizzy Burden reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Iran Ratchets Up Talk of Controlling Hormuz Before New Talks
title_cn: 伊朗在新会谈之前加强控制霍尔木兹海峡的讨论
summary: Iran reiterated its determination to control maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, raising the stakes ahead of fresh talks in Qatar on formally ending its war with the US.

title: Iran Says it Will Control Hormuz & Has No Plans to Meet US in Qatar | Daybreak Europe 6/30/2026
title_cn: 伊朗表示将控制霍尔木兹且没有计划在卡塔尔与美国会面欧洲黎明 2026 年 6 月 30 日
summary:
Bloomberg Daybreak Europe is your essential morning viewing to stay ahead. Live from London, we set the agenda for your day, catching you up with overnight markets news from the US and Asia. And we'll tell you what matters for investors in Europe, giving you insight before trading begins.

On today's show, stocks in Asia headed for their biggest quarterly jump since 2009, as investors piled back into companies seen as beneficiaries of the AI buildout. Elsewhere, the yen extended its recent losses to weaken beyond 162 per dollar.

A top Iranian official says Iran is determined to maintain control over maritime traffic in the Strait of Hormuz, raising the stakes ahead of talks, which the US says will take place in Doha on Tuesday. 

And the ECB President Christine Lagarde has lauded Europe's resilience to economic shocks in her opening speech at the ECB's annual forum in Sintra, Portugal. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: EU, China Set October Deadline to Defuse Rising Trade Tensions
title_cn: 欧盟和中国设定十月最后期限以化解日益加剧的贸易紧张局势
summary: The European Union and China have set an October deadline to resolve ongoing trade disputes. Following a meeting with Chinese Commerce Minister Wang Wentao in Brussels, EU trade chief Maros Sefcovic stated that while not all issues will be fixed, both sides have enough time to achieve "tangible results." Bloomberg's Richard Bravo reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: South Africa's Police on High Alert for Anti-Migrant Protests
title_cn: 南非警方对反移民抗议活动保持高度戒备
summary: South African police warned they would tolerate no intimidation or violence as they prepare for Tuesday's nationwide anti-migrant demonstrations. The demonstrations are the culmination of weeks of protests that have displaced thousands of mainly African expatriates. Bloomberg's Jennifer Zabasajja reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Buffett Delays Gifts to Gates’ Charity Amid Epstein Review: WSJ
title_cn: 华尔街日报：在爱泼斯坦的批评中，巴菲特推迟了向盖茨慈善机构捐赠的礼物
summary: Warren Buffett has decided to skip his midyear donations to the Gates Foundation for the first time in two decades, as he waits for the results of a review into the organization’s links to late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, the Wall Street Journal reported late Monday.

title: EU and China Agree on October Deadline to Reset Trade Ties
title_cn: 欧盟和中国就重启贸易关系的十月最后期限达成一致
summary: The European Union and China set an October deadline to make progress on trade disagreements, as tensions rise between the two economic powers.
